subject: Physics
title: Lodestones: Ancient Magnets
description: A micro-lesson that makes Lodestones: Ancient Magnets usable.

hook.text: Ancient Greeks discovered a rock near the city of Magnesia that attracted iron. They called it magnetite.

buildup.text: Lodestones are naturally magnetized pieces of magnetite. They're the only natural permanent magnets on Earth.

discovery.text: Chinese navigators were the first to use lodestones as compasses around 200 BC, revolutionizing exploration.

twist.text: Lodestones are rare because they need to be struck by lightning to become permanently magnetized.

climax.text: From a curious Greek rock to global navigation—magnetism changed how humanity explored the world.

punchline.text: Lightning strikes rock. Rock points north. Exploration begins.

quiz.question: What makes lodestones naturally magnetic?
options: They are magnetite that was struck by lightning
They contain pure iron at their core
They were formed near Earth's magnetic poles
